TERMS OF USE

Terms of Use for talnyc.com

Last Updated: May 14, 2026

Welcome to talnyc.com (the “Site”), the official website for TAL Bistro & Bar (the “Venue”). These Terms of Use (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of the Site and its content, features, and services (collectively, the “Services”).

PLEASE READ THESE TERMS CAREFULLY. By accessing or using our Site, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Site.

1. Description of Services

The Site provides information about our Venue, including but not limited to, our menu, pricing, business hours, location, and special events. The Site also provides functionality for you to make reservations for the Venue, which may be processed directly by us or through a third-party service.

2. Permitted Use

You may use the Site solely for your personal or corporate use to view information and make legitimate reservations. You agree to use the Site only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms.

3. Prohibited Use

You agree not to:

  • Use the Site in any way that violates any applicable federal, state, local, or international law or regulation.
  • Use any robot, spider, scraper, or other automated means to access the Site for any purpose without our express written permission.
  • Interfere with or disrupt the operation of the Site or the servers or networks used to make the Site available.
  • Use the Site to make any speculative, false, or fraudulent reservations.
  • Reproduce, duplicate, copy, sell, resell, or otherwise exploit any portion of the Site, its content, or access to the Services for any commercial purpose.

4. No User Accounts

You acknowledge that the Site does not offer or require user accounts. All access to the Site’s informational content is publicly available without registration.

5. Reservations and Third-Party Services

When you make a reservation through our Site, you may be providing information directly to us or to a third-party reservation service (e.g., Square, Yelp, OpenTable, Google). If you are redirected to a third-party website, your reservation and any information you provide will be governed by that third party’s terms of use and privacy policy. We are not responsible for the practices, terms, or policies of any third-party services.

6. SMS Messaging

By providing your mobile number on our forms and checking the SMS consent box, you agree to receive recurring SMS messages from TAL Bistro & Bar regarding reservations, customer care, and occasional marketing promotions. Message frequency varies. Msg & data rates may apply. Reply HELP for help, STOP to opt out. See our Privacy Policy for details on how your information is used. Your mobile information will not be shared with third parties or affiliates for marketing or promotional purposes.

7. Intellectual Property

All content on the Site, including text, graphics, logos, images (such as menu items or venue photos), and software, is the property of TAL Bistro & Bar or its licensors (unless otherwise stated) and is protected by United States and international copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws. You may not use any of our trademarks or service marks without our prior written consent.

8. Disclaimers

The Site and its content are provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis, without any warranties of any kind, either express or implied.

While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, we do not warrant that information on the Site (including, but not limited to, menu items, pricing, and business hours) is complete, reliable, current, or error-free. All information is subject to change without notice.

9. Limitation of Liability

To the fullest extent permitted by law, in no event will TAL Bistro & Bar, its affiliates, or their licensors, service providers, employees, agents, officers, or directors be liable for any damages of any kind, under any legal theory, arising out of or in connection with your use, or inability to use, the Site, any content on the Site, or any services obtained through the Site.

10. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to revise and update these Terms at any time in our sole discretion. All changes are effective immediately when we post them. Your continued use of the Site following the posting of revised Terms means that you accept and agree to the changes.

11. Governing Law

These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the United States and the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

12. Contact Us

If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact us at hello@talnyc.com.

TAL Menu Items 2026-04-08
TAL Cocktail Menu 2026-02-25

탈

KOREAN BISTRO

Experience refined Asian hospitality in an atmosphere where tradition and modernity blend seamlessly. Sip, savor, and unwind in elegance.

Reserve Your Table

TAL Korean Bistro is where the essence of Korea’s heritage comes to life. Drawing inspiration from the symbolic tal mask, our bistro weaves tradition into every detail—from authentic dishes and artisanal drinks to a warm, inviting ambiance where culture meets modern elegance.

 

BENEFITS OF

MAKGEOLLI

Explore the unique perks of makgeolli, a traditional Korean rice wine steeped in cultural heritage. With its creamy texture and subtly sweet taste, makgeolli is not just tasty but also nutrient-rich. Known for its probiotic benefits, it supports gut health and aids digestion. Packed with vitamins and minerals, this delightful drink can boost your wellness while offering a refreshing experience.

meaning behind

TAL Korean mask

Korean masks, known as "tal (탈),” are not merely disguises but are deeply embedded in Korean culture, representing a transformation of identity and connection to the divine, supernatural, or mythical. They are central to traditional performances like "Talchum (탈춤)" and "Talnori (탈놀이)," which involve mask dances and dramas that often satirize societal issues and express cultural values.
